item.title: I have too many memes: Dump #135 entirely anti-capitalism
item.description:
This one is kind of depressing/maddening
I didn’t realise I had this many of these.
This might be the entire next dump as well
I also feel the need to say I’m not American so a lot of this does not apply to me
I’m Australian, and we are definitely better than the US.
But not as good as I’d like
From what I’ve seen you guys have been there for a few years
I also spent all day removing watermarks to the best of my ability cause people keep complaining. If I don’t see one thank you I will go back to leaving them in. Thank you :]
item.dc:creator: Sweenerd

item.title: Everyone should learn about this
item.description:
*we also created other smaller Nuclear plants as well but Bruce is the largest* they all contributed to displacing Coal
There is a lot to be said about climate change as well. But I think a lot of the time we focus on Climate change and not the direct impacts to health in cities like Toronto - Many argue that Geography plays a role in their emissions as well. Ontario has some natural advantages with Hydro but we also have a lot of disadvantages like hard winters that make renewables less viable. Every region has advantages and disadvantages.
item.dc:creator: McKittyNuts

item.title: A fresh batch of memes from the meme wagon.
item.description:
https://open.spotify.com/playlist/3svq1RBHcMjYEaRua5iovx?si=08LyLfxnSVSeI2gIKv443w&pi=x6CnTscDQDun3
Hey, it’s me yo boy Tal, What motivates me to make these meme dumps you say? Well it’s to coax you into listening to my band. I’ll take a muted listen as well fam, just turn the volum down and I’ll still get the plays. Perhaps someday I can use the fake numbers to play on a real festival. Xoxo https://open.spotify.com/artist/19ptBf1ldxSQLo60aDiso7?si=yONGOVTAS4Wt5jLFOH2Srw

AnonymousMockingjay

item.title: 4 years as a steel worker
item.description: I just want to tell someone. I know for most people, its completely normal to have worked full time since early adulthood. That having a job is no big deal. I was pretty much given up on. I couldnt hold a job. My mental health was just a mess, and i truly thought there was no hope for me. But then i was given a chance at this steel factory, 4 years ago. I was supposed to try it out for 3 months, and i was gonna work maaaaybe 60%. 4 years later, and i have worked 100% for 3.5 years of those. I have never held a job for this long, and i feel so incredibly proud. Words can not describe how much it means for those who struggle, to be given a chance. I thrive with what i do, and i have grown in so many ways. I have learned how to push myself, but also how to take care of myself. I have discovered strength, dedication and skills i never thought i had. In july last year, i was ~finally~ diagnosed with autism, which has made a lot of sense. A bit bitter, being this late diagnosed(i am 38), but also better late than never...? What i do, mostly, is making what they pour steel into. I opreate two machines, that makes the casts out of sand, binder, and heat(250°C, ca). We make a lot of different products, so there is never a dull day. I feel great pride and joy in making the best casts possible. Casts that are safe for the steel at 1600°C to be poured into. (And even tho the shirt says "boss", i am in NO way a boss. Its just our trademarked product, mainly things used on fishingboats etc). Thanks for reading :)
item.dc:creator: dotnote

item.title: Budapest just held the largest pride in history despite police ban
item.description:
I wanted to share what happened yesterday at Budapest Pride, because I don't see enough coverage for it, even though it was a HUGE win for my country, a beautiful day to show that there is always hope and I'm happy that I was part of it! We showed the world what it truly means to be an ally!
In March, Orban's government passed a law that allowed the ban of pride, posing as a "child protection law". Organizers of the Budapest Pride vowed to continue with the annual festival despite the new law and declaring: “We will fight this new fascist ban.”
A few weeks ago, an official ban was issued: organizing or attending could lead to legal consequences, including a 500 euro fine and possible criminal charges.
The mayor of Budapest, who's openly opposed to Orban's regime, showed support and announced that he's working with the organizers to pass the event as a protest, which made the police ban invalid under the rights of assembly.
But the police ban remained in effect. Facial recognition was deployed to identify participants along the planned route of the parade. The use of drones was banned except for police surveillance ones (a big part of why you don't see air footage showing the real size of gatherings). Additionally, far-right groups received permits to hold counter-demonstrations at the same time, blocking a bridge that was part of the planned route.
They did everything to scare us, and they failed. The crowd of around 200 000 people marched on, it was the largest pride in our history. It was an incredible experience to march in a crowd as large as 1/10th of the city. The pictures that exist don't do it justice. It was heartwarming to see how many people are willing to stand up for democracy and the protection of queer people. I finally see a slim hope of dismantling this 15 years old regime on our back!
item.dc:creator: MightyStuk

item.title: Sunday Dump of amazing and funny things. Enjoy !
item.description:
1. Tossed him like a ragdoll
2. She's a fairy.
3. Great age and treachery.
4. Short man passing through turn gate.
5. Now that's a gaming setup.
6. Fun Woodpecker Bell.
7. Some sort of race with wooden blocks. She's really good at it.
8. Did you lock your keys inside ?
9. Nice catch.
10. She encountered a fairy .
11. This Hydraulic Tripod.
12. Learn Morse code in 45 seconds !
13. The coolest restaurant in Japan.
14. Slate roof tiles.
15. A demonstration of how to untangle using topology.
16. This man build a LEGO city.
17. This is business.
18. How Fake Money Is Cut with Precision.
19. Don't feed beans to ducks.
20. RC Car Football.
21. A breathtaking view of Antarctica with sunlit ice and endless horizons.
22. A bartender in India.
23. Amazing Landing.
24. Valve seats installation.
25. Shopping cart washer.
26. The Builder.
27. Sometimes, you gotta do what you gotta do.
28. Physics and art make for a powerful combination.
29. How incredible for the kids to be welcomed by their idols.
30. Airlifting of high power electrical transmission tower.
31. Tree Hugger In Action.
32. Indian man performs an underwater pendulum.
33. Those guys show how magic is done.
34. WTF with that reaction ?
35. Shut up and take my money.
36. A craftsman from Vietnam has assembled an unusual car.

item.title: What Happened Today #149
item.description: The cracks are finally showing. Louisiana hospitals are begging their own Republican leaders to stop the "historic devastation," while Trump threatens to primary Tillis for protecting North Carolina from Medicaid cuts. When Republicans start getting scared enough to break ranks with their own party, the damage has become too obvious to hide. Call your representatives now and demand they leverage every one of these cracks before Republicans close ranks again. Don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you always know what's going on. Add your email at govbrief dot today or find us on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com 1. https://go.govbrief.today/senate-gop-advance-bill 2. https://go.govbrief.today/hortman-funeral 3. https://go.govbrief.today/roberts-warns-violence 4. https://go.govbrief.today/g7-corp-minimum-tax 5. https://go.govbrief.today/felon-released-testify-kilmar 6. https://go.govbrief.today/usip-reverts-to-trump 7. https://go.govbrief.today/louisiana-health-warn-johnson-scalise 8. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-primary-tillis 9. https://go.govbrief.today/voa-employee-firings-rescinded Yesterday's Story - https://go.govbrief.today/scotus-voids-national-injunctions Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/alligator-alcatraz-protest Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
